data.title: Long Island's Nassau County to Issue $277.7M of General Improvement Bonds
data.description:
By Chris Wack

The County of Nassau, N.Y., is selling $277.7 million in general improvement bonds to finance projects throughout the county.

The offering will consist of 2025 Series A bonds, according to a document posted Thursday on MuniOS.

The bonds will be sold in a competitive sale, and bids will be accepted until 10:00 a.m. ET on May 14.

The bonds are special obligations backed by a pledge of the county's faith and credit. All taxable real property in the county is subject to the levy of taxes, which will pay interest and principal on the debt.

Interest on the bonds is payable starting on Oct. 1. They will mature starting April 1, 2026 through April 1, 2055. Interest rates and yields have not yet be determined.

Nassau County in located in New York State, on Long Island, and has a population of about 1.4 million.

Moody's has rated the bonds Aa2 with a stable outlook, while Fitch and S&P Global have assigned a rating of AA with a stable outlook.

PFM Financial Advisors is listed as the offering's lead manager.

Write to Chris Wack at chris.wack@wsj.com

data.title: Ex Fed's Warsh highlights a path to lower rates, takes a fresh dig at the Fed
data.description:
Key points:
  • Warsh says Fed would sacrifice the labor market to fight inflation
  • Warsh is seen as a front-runner to be the next Fed Chair
  • Remarks made at Hoover Institution, linked to Bush-Reagan Republicanism

Kevin Warsh, an apparent frontrunner to be U.S. President Donald Trump's pick to be the next chair of the Federal Reserve, on Friday suggested a possible pathway to the lower policy rates that Trump has repeatedly pressed the current Fed Chair Jerome Powell to deliver, and delivered a fresh dig at the Fed's conduct of monetary policy.

A large and often growing Fed balance sheet can work at cross-purposes with the Fed's main policy lever of setting short-term borrowing rates, Warsh told a monetary policy panel at Stanford University's Hoover Institution, but "if the printing press could be quiet, we could have lower policy rates."

Warsh served as Fed Governor from 2006 until 2011, when he quit because he opposed the Fed's continued balance sheet expansion as central bank overreach that encouraged the expansion of the nation's debt. The Fed is currently reducing its balance sheet.

On Friday, Warsh offered an added criticism of the Fed, saying there is "cruel choice" between the Fed's two objectives of stable prices and full employment, a reference to the idea long prevalent among many central bank policymakers that the cost of bringing down inflation is harm to the job market.

"What it means is, we don't have to push the unemployment rate up to get the inflation rate to fall," Warsh said on the sidelines of the conference. "At the Fed, when they talk about how we get inflation down, what they really mean is, how do we get the unemployment rate up...we need to throw people out of work to get the inflation rate to come down, which is nonsense. But that's embedded in economic thinking, including at the Fed."

The idea that a "cruel choice" is "nonsense" is actually largely consistent with how the Fed has conducted policy over the last several years, as it brought inflation down from 40-year highs without pushing the unemployment rate above the rate that most economists feel is consistent with full employment.

Powell has repeatedly said he does not feel the current labor market is a source of inflation, suggesting that crushing the job market would do little to lower inflation.

Warsh made his remarks at a conference and an institution steeped in Bush-Reagan Republicanism, now out of favor as Trump and his ideas have come to dominate the party. The conference was convened in part to celebrate John Taylor, a Bush economic adviser and author of one of the most famous monetary policy rules; Condoleeza Rice, Bush's Secretary of State, spoke on Thursday.

Warsh himself was a Bush appointee to the Fed but also has close family ties to Trump through his wife — daughter of Trump former donor Ronald Lauder. He shared the stage Friday with Fed Governor Christopher Waller, a Trump appointee who has also been mentioned as a possible candidate for Fed chair. Waller has said he's prepared to lower rates swiftly should tariffs drive a slowdown in the economy and send the unemployment rate upward.

Asked what the Fed should do now, if its inflation and employment mandates come into tension, Warsh demurred.

"Well, that's a longer discussion," he said, heading back into the conference to hear the next speakers.

data.title: AgriBank Reports First Quarter 2025 Financial Results
data.description:
AgriBank Reports First Quarter 2025 Financial Results

PR Newswire

ST. PAUL, Minn., May 9, 2025

Continued strong financial performance reflects model, Association success

ST. PAUL, Minn., May 9, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Today, St. Paul-based AgriBank announced financial results for the first quarter of 2025, with strong profitability, credit quality, and liquidity and capital.

Highlights:

  • Profitability: Net income remained strong at $242.6 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025. AgriBank's year-to-date return on assets (ROA) ratio of 51 basis points was above the target of 50 basis points.
  • Credit quality: Total loan portfolio credit quality remained strong, with 99.4 percent of loans classified as acceptable at March 31, 2025.
  • Liquidity and capital: End-of-the-quarter liquidity was 160 days, well above the regulatory requirement. Capital also remained well above the regulatory minimums and company targets.

"Our strong financial performance in the first quarter of 2025 is a testament to the resilience and dedication of our team and the success of our Farm Credit Association-owners," said AgriBank CEO Jeffrey Swanhorst. "AgriBank and the Associations stand ready to support farmers, ranchers, and other Farm Credit borrowers navigate what we expect will be continued economic and market uncertainty and volatility, bolstered by the smart business practices of the producers themselves."

2025 Results of Operations

Net interest income was $269.6 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025, an increase of $34.0 million, or 14.4 percent, compared to the same period of the prior year. The increase was primarily driven by higher spread income and increased volume in AgriBank's wholesale loan portfolio. Additionally, the benefit of equity financing contributed slightly to the increase in net interest income. These factors were somewhat offset by decreased spread income on investment securities due to the mix of investment securities and reduced spreads on money market instruments.

Non-interest income was $29.0 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025, an increase of $415.0 thousand, or 1.4 percent, compared to the same period of the prior year, primarily related to an Allocated Insurance Reserve Accounts (AIRAs) distribution received from the Farm Credit System Insurance Corporation (FCSIC) during the first quarter of 2025. The increase in non-interest income from AIRAs was partially offset by a reduction in mineral income.

Non-interest expense was $55.1 million for the three months ended March 31, 2025, an increase of $3.6 million, or 7.0 percent, compared to the same period of the prior year. The increase was mainly due to dealer incentive expenses related to AgriBank's crop input financing portfolio.

Loan Portfolio

Total loans were $164.7 billion at March 31, 2025, a decrease of $7.3 million, compared to December 31, 2024. This decrease was primarily attributable to retail loan repayments, largely offset by wholesale loan growth.

AgriBank's credit quality reflects the overall financial strength of District Associations and their underlying portfolios of retail loans. AgriBank's portfolio was composed of 99.4 percent acceptable loans at March 31, 2025, and December 31, 2024. Loans classified as acceptable represent the highest-quality assets. The credit quality of AgriBank's retail loan portfolio decreased slightly to 95.1 percent classified as acceptable at March 31, 2025, compared to 95.7 percent acceptable at December 31, 2024.

Agricultural Conditions

On February 6, 2025, the U.S. Department of Agriculture's Economic Research Service released its initial forecast of the U.S. aggregate farm income and financial conditions for 2025 and updated its 2024 forecast. The revised 2024 net farm income forecast of $139.1 billion represented an $8.2 billion decline from the 2023 level, down 5.6 percent, and follows the $34.7 billion estimated decline that occurred in 2023 compared to the record-high 2022 estimated net farm income. Although net farm income is forecasted to decline for the second consecutive year, when adjusting for inflation, the 2024 net farm income forecast is $16.8 billion, or 13.3 percent, above the 10-year average (2014-2023) net farm income in 2025 dollars. The initial 2025 net farm income projection of $180.1 billion represents an increase of $41.0 billion, or 29.5 percent, from the revised 2024 net farm income forecast, and if realized, it would be the second-highest inflation-adjusted level in the past 50 years.

Many factors, including weather, trade, government, and monetary policy, global agricultural production levels, and pathogenic outbreaks in livestock and poultry, may keep the agriculture market volatility elevated. Implementation of cost-saving technologies, marketing methods, and risk management strategies will continue to cause a wide range of results among the respective agricultural producers.

Capital Resources and Liquidity

Total capital remained strong at $9.6 billion as of March 31, 2025, an increase of $88.5 million compared to December 31, 2024. The increase was driven primarily by AgriBank's net income, which was partially offset by cash patronage declared, consistent with AgriBank's capital plan. AgriBank exceeded all regulatory capital minimum requirements, including additional regulatory buffers.

Cash, cash equivalents, and investments totaled $25.8 billion and $25.1 billion at March 31, 2025, and December 31, 2024, respectively. AgriBank's end-of-the-period liquidity position represented 160 days coverage of maturing debt obligations, which supports operational demands, and was well above the 90-day minimum established by AgriBank's regulator.

About AgriBank

AgriBank is part of the customer-owned, nationwide Farm Credit System. Under Farm Credit's cooperative structure, AgriBank is primarily owned by local Farm Credit Associations, which provide financial products and services to rural communities and agriculture. AgriBank obtains funds and provides funding and financial solutions to those Associations. AgriBank and those Associations compose the AgriBank District. The District covers a 15-state area stretching from Wyoming to Ohio and Minnesota to Arkansas. For more information, visit www.AgriBank.com.

Forward-Looking Statements

Any forward-looking statements in this press release are based on current expectations and are subject to uncertainty and changes in circumstances. Actual results may differ materially from expectations due to a number of risks and uncertainties. More information about these risks and uncertainties is contained in AgriBank's annual report, which is available approximately 75 days following the end of the year. AgriBank undertakes no duty to update or revise any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise.

data.title: Wolfspeed is uncertain about its future. Its stock drop shows investors are, too.
data.description:
By Britney Nguyen

The chip company is looking to slash costs, improve its finances and cope with growth woes

Wolfspeed Inc. reported fiscal third-quarter earnings in line with expectations, but uncertainty over a restructuring of its finances has investors worried.

On Wolfspeed's (WOLF) earnings call after the bell Thursday, the company said it is focused on improving its finances, and has "engaged external experts to continue to identify additional cost-saving measures" in addition to current efforts, including cutting 30% of its senior leadership team.

Wolfspeed makes silicon-carbide technology that's used in the automotive industry, but analysts at Mizuho said that muted demand for electric vehicles in the U.S. and Europe is pinching the company's growth prospects.

See more: Wolfspeed posts revenue beat, cites 'significant progress' on new operating plan

The chip company's stock was down 25% during midday trading on Friday, after it reported its March-quarter results and offered no guidance for the June quarter. Wolfspeed reported total revenue of $185 million for the fiscal third quarter - down more than 7% from the previous year - and $78 million in revenue for its Mohawk Valley fabrication facility.

TD Cowen analysts suspended their rating and price target on the stock because of the company's "increasing likelihood of financial restructuring," they said in a Thursday note.

"Given uncertainty regarding the company's capital structure and the impact on earnings going forward, we are unable to establish a valuation framework at this time, and suspend our rating, price target and estimates," TD Cowen said.

On the company's earnings call, Wolfspeed said it doesn't anticipate its debt negotiations to materially impact stakeholders, but that because it "may elect to pursue either in-court or out-of-court options" as part of the negotiations, the company expects to add "going-concern language in the footnotes to the financial statements" of its next 10-Q regulatory filing.

The disclosure is "perhaps a sign of looming liquidation," analysts at Susquehanna International Group said in a note on Friday.

And Wolfspeed's 8-K filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission "revealing it would not pursue a proposed transaction (involving equitization) as part of negotiations with its creditors" is worrying, Susquehanna analysts added.

Susquehanna reduced its top-line forecast for fiscal-year 2026 to $850 million from $962 million and cut its forecast for fiscal-year 2027 to $1 billion from $1.3 billion, after reviewing the long-term model in Wolfspeed's 8-K filing.

Wolfspeed Chief Executive Robert Feurle said on the earnings call that the company needs to focus on serving its blue-chip customers with its fully automated, 200-millimeter silicon-carbide solutions manufacturing platform, which "sets us apart."

However, Susquehanna analysts said they "continue to worry about [Wolfspeed's] ability to compete with Chinese substrates" as the chip company ramps up its silicon-carbide technology manufacturing.

Meanwhile, Wolfspeed said it is holding "constructive dialogue" over federal funding from the Biden administration's 2022 Chips Act with the program's office and the Trump administration.

The chip company is expecting to receive $750 million in federal funding, which was designed to bring more of the semiconductor supply chain stateside.

"Wolfspeed is positioned to help achieve some of this administration's important initiatives, leading the American semiconductor revolution, reassuring the manufacturing of critical mineral derivatives, boosting domestic production of technologies critical to national security and securing domestic supply chain," Wolfspeed Chairman Tom Werner said on the company's earnings call.

Citi analysts downgraded Wolfspeed's stock to sell and lowered estimates, citing "significant financial challenges in a sluggish macro environment," including financial losses and concerns over its Chips Act funding.

Wolfspeed announced Friday morning that it was appointing Paul Walsh and Mark Jensen, who have both served as chief financial officers elsewhere, to its board of directors.

"Their backgrounds position them well to help the board and company navigate ongoing discussions with lenders and reach a comprehensive solution to address our balance sheet," Werner said in a release.

The company's stock is down 50% so far this year.

-Britney Nguyen

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: McKesson's Fiscal Q4 'More Loaded Than Normal' Amid Spin-Off News, UBS Says
data.description:
McKesson's fiscal Q4 update was "more loaded than normal," UBS Securities said in a note Friday, as the company announced plans to spin off its Medical-Surgical Solutions business and issued initial fiscal 2026 guidance broadly in line with expectations.

The company said it will separate the Med-Surg segment into a standalone entity, with further details on timing and structure to follow. UBS said the value creation from the proposed spin is "not immediately clear," as it would likely require the remaining company to trade at a higher earnings multiple to unlock value.

The brokerage characterized McKesson's Q4 results as operationally in line, with better performance in the Pharmaceutical segment offsetting weaker Med-Surg results and most of the upside driven by below-the-line items such as interest and tax rates.

McKesson reported adjusted EPS of $10.12 for fiscal Q4, topping the FactSet consensus of $9.83. Revenue rose to $90.82 billion for the quarter ended March 31, below analyst estimates of $94.30 billion.

The company also introduced fiscal 2026 EPS guidance of $36.75 to $37.55, implying 11% to 14% year-over-year growth. UBS called the guidance in line with both its own forecast and buy-side expectations.

Looking ahead, the firm flagged that near-term stock performance may hinge less on fundamentals and more on regulatory developments, particularly the expected announcement of Most Favored Nation drug pricing rules for Medicare.

McKesson's oncology and MSO businesses could be exposed if the average sales price for Part B drugs are lowered, although UBS expects some relief measures for clinicians.

The firm has a buy rating on the stock with a $805 price target.

data.title: RealReal Stock Drops After Recording Slower Consignment Sales Growth
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Shares of RealReal were in retreat after the San Francisco-based company posted slower sales growth in its core consignment business that missed Wall Street's expectations for the latest quarter.

The stock fell 33% to $4.88 on Friday. Shares have fallen 55% this year.

RealReal said after the close on Thursday that first quarter core consignment revenue growth was 7.1%, down from 13% growth in the fourth quarter and 14% in the prior year. The company recorded core consignment revenue of $123.8 million in the quarter, missing the $134.4 million expected by analysts, according to FactSet.

The marketplace for the resale of luxury goods posted a profit of $62.4 million, or a loss of 14 cents a share, compared to a loss of $31.1 million, or 30 cents a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted losses per share was 8 cents, which was better than the adjusted loss of 10 cents a share analysts expected, according to FactSet.

Revenue rose 11% to $160 million. Analysts polled by FactSet expected $159.8 million.

The company maintained its full-year sales guidance of $645 million to $660 million, up from $600.5 million in 2024. Analysts expect $654.7 million.

Chief Executive Rati Levesque said the reaffirmed guidance comes despite uncertainties from tariffs.

"We occupy a unique position at the intersection of luxury and value, and we source our supply primarily from domestic closets, so there is potential to realize benefits in the current environment," Levesque said.

For the second quarter, the company guided for revenue of $157 million to $161 million, up from $144.9 million a year earlier. Analysts expect $159 million.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: WESTAMERICA BANCORPORATION SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Westamerica Bancorporation, a prominent regional bank primarily serving Northern and Central California, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting the challenges and strategic initiatives undertaken in the current economic environment.

Financial Highlights

Net Interest and Fee Income (FTE): $56.39 million, reflecting a decrease from the previous year due to lower interest income from debt securities.

Net Income: $31.04 million, a decline compared to the same period last year, primarily due to reduced interest income.

Basic Earnings Per Common Share: $1.16, down from $1.37 in the prior year, indicating a decrease in profitability per share.

Diluted Earnings Per Common Share: $1.16, consistent with basic earnings, showing no dilution effect from potential common stock equivalents.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company’s operations are managed and financial performance is evaluated on a company-wide basis, with loans, investments, and deposits providing the primary revenue streams.

Geographical Performance: The company primarily extends loans to commercial and consumer customers in Northern and Central California, indicating a regional focus in its operations.

Future Outlook: Management continues to evaluate the impacts of inflation, Federal Reserve monetary policy, tariffs, international trade tensions, and climate changes on the company’s business. The banking industry could experience significant volatility, as seen with several regional bank failures in 2023, which could affect liquidity, deposit outflows, and unrealized losses on investment debt securities.

Operational Risks: The company is exposed to various qualitative risks, including economic and market conditions, borrowers’ business performance, and the value of collateral for secured loans. These factors are considered in the allowance for credit losses and the overall risk management strategy.

Loan Portfolio Management: The company’s loan review department performs independent evaluations of loans and validates management-assigned credit risk grades, ensuring a robust risk management framework.

Credit Risk Management: The company maintains a systematic methodology to estimate loss potential, with loans segregated into pools based on common characteristics for more accurate risk assessment.

Allowance for Credit Losses: The company’s allowance for credit losses is maintained at a level considered adequate to provide for expected losses based on historical loss rates adjusted for current and expected conditions over a forecast period.

SEC Filing: WESTAMERICA BANCORPORATION [ WABC ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: Sylvamo Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Sylvamo Corp, a leading global producer of uncoated papers, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial and operational performance, reflecting the challenges and strategic initiatives undertaken during the period.

Financial Highlights

Net Sales: $821 million, reflecting a decrease from $905 million in the prior year, primarily due to lower volumes in North America and decreased sales price and mix in Europe.

Income Before Income Taxes: $33 million, down from $60 million in the previous year, impacted by lower sales and higher planned maintenance outage costs.

Net Income: $27 million, compared to $43 million in the first quarter of 2024, with a diluted EPS of $0.65, down from $1.02, reflecting decreased sales and increased costs.

Adjusted EBITDA: $90 million, with an adjusted EBITDA margin of 11%, compared to $118 million and a margin of 13% in the first quarter of 2024, indicating less favorable input and transportation costs.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company's net sales for the first quarter of 2025 were $821 million, with a breakdown by region showing $190 million from Europe, $193 million from Latin America, and $438 million from North America. The sales in Europe and Latin America decreased compared to the previous year, primarily due to lower sales prices, mix, and volumes.

Geographical Performance: In Europe, sales decreased by $17 million due to lower sales prices, mix, and volumes, along with higher outage and input costs. Latin America saw a $17 million decrease in sales, impacted by lower volumes and unfavorable foreign exchange, but operating profit increased due to lower operating and outage costs. North America experienced a $52 million decrease in sales, primarily due to lower volumes, with operating profit affected by higher operating and input costs.

Sales Units: The North America segment faced a significant decrease in sales volume, contributing to a $52 million reduction in sales compared to the previous year. This was primarily due to the closure of International Paper’s Georgetown mill and operational challenges.

Future Outlook: Looking ahead to the second quarter of 2025, the company expects favorable price and mix in Latin America and North America, stable volume, and improved operations and costs due to seasonally lower costs in Europe and North America. However, planned maintenance outage costs are projected to increase by $36 million as the company executes the heaviest outage quarter of the year across all three regions.

SEC Filing: Sylvamo Corp [ SLVM ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: OP Bancorp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
OP Bancorp, a financial institution primarily serving the Korean-American communities within California and other states, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting its strategic focus and market conditions.

Financial Highlights

  • Interest Income: $34.9 million, reflecting a 5.9% increase from the previous year, driven by higher interest and fees on loans.
  • Net Interest Income: $17.4 million, an increase of 9.0% from the previous year, indicating improved interest margins despite rising interest expenses.
  • Net Income: $5.6 million, a 6.4% increase from the previous year, showcasing effective cost management and increased noninterest income.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share: $0.37, up from $0.34, reflecting the company's ability to generate higher earnings per share.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share: $0.37, consistent with basic earnings per share, indicating no significant dilution from potential share conversions.

Business Highlights

  • Geographical Performance: OP Bancorp operates primarily in the Korean-American communities within California, with branches in Los Angeles, Orange Counties, Santa Clara, Carrollton, Texas, and near Las Vegas, Nevada. The company also has loan production offices in Pleasanton, California, Atlanta, Georgia, Aurora, Colorado, Lynnwood, Washington, and Fairfax, Virginia.
  • Operational Focus: The company has been focusing on liquidity and capital management due to recent economic conditions, which has limited growth and near-term profitability. This strategic shift is in response to the challenges posed by fluctuating interest rates and competitive pressures.
  • Customer Segments: OP Bancorp primarily serves small and medium-sized businesses, their owners, and retail customers, with a significant focus on the Korean-American market within its primary geographical areas.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates continued challenges due to the volatile political climate and uncertain economic conditions, which make it difficult to predict interest rate adjustments by the Federal Reserve. Management is focused on adapting to these conditions while maintaining customer loyalty and operational efficiency.

SEC Filing: OP Bancorp [ OPBK ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: CVB FINANCIAL CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
CVB Financial Corp, operating primarily through its wholly owned subsidiary Citizens Business Bank, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational highlights, reflecting its strategic focus on traditional banking activities and effective cost management amidst a challenging economic environment.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $143.0 million, reflecting a decrease from the previous year due to lower interest income from loans and leases.
  • Net Interest Income: $110.4 million, slightly down from the previous year, indicating stable interest margins despite a challenging interest rate environment.
  • Net Earnings: $51.1 million, an increase from the previous year, driven by effective cost management and a recapture of provision for credit losses.
  • Basic Earnings Per Common Share: $0.37, showing growth compared to the previous year, supported by improved net earnings.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Common Share: $0.36, consistent with the increase in net earnings, reflecting the company's ability to maintain profitability.

Business Highlights

  • Business Operations: CVB Financial Corp. operates primarily through its wholly owned subsidiary, Citizens Business Bank, focusing on traditional banking activities such as accepting deposits and lending money. The Bank also provides trust and investment-related services through its CitizensTrust Division.
  • Geographical Performance: The Bank's operations are concentrated in California, with 62 banking centers and three trust office locations as of March 31, 2025. The customer base primarily consists of small to mid-sized businesses and individuals throughout the state.
  • Loan Portfolio Composition: As of March 31, 2025, 81.02% of the Company's total loan portfolio consisted of real estate loans, with commercial real estate loans representing 77.6% of total loans. The real estate loans and construction loans are primarily secured by properties located in California.
  • Credit Quality Management: The Company employs a loan risk rating system to monitor credit quality, categorizing loans into Pass, Special Mention, Substandard, Doubtful, and Loss. This system helps in assessing the adequacy of the allowance for credit losses.
  • Allowance for Credit Losses: The allowance for credit losses (ACL) was $78.3 million at March 31, 2025, reflecting a decrease from $80.1 million at December 31, 2024. The ACL as a percentage of total loans and leases was 0.94% at both March 31, 2025, and December 31, 2024.
  • Future Outlook: Management anticipates continued economic challenges due to inflationary pressures, high interest rates, and lower commercial real estate values. The economic forecast includes a slower GDP growth rate and a rise in unemployment, which may impact future provisions for credit losses.
  • Operational Strategy: The Company operates as one reportable segment, focusing on providing relationship-based banking products and services to commercial clients. The strategic approach includes leveraging a company-wide offering of similar products and services managed through consistent processes and platforms.

SEC Filing: CVB FINANCIAL CORP [ CVBF ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: Aeluma, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Aeluma, Inc., a leading innovator in the semiconductor industry, has released its Form 10-Q report for the first quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial improvements and key business developments, driven primarily by government contracts and strategic operational initiatives. This summary provides an overview of the company's financial performance and business activities for the period ending March 31, 2025.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $3.35 million, increased by $2.71 million compared to the same period in 2024, primarily driven by government contracts.
  • Operating Expenses: $4.52 million, increased by 7.3% from the same period in 2024, mainly due to higher salaries and employee benefits.
  • Net Income (Loss): $(2.16) million, improved by $1.41 million from the same period in 2024, reflecting increased revenue and cost management.
  • Basic Net Income (Loss) Per Share: $(0.18), compared to $(0.29) in the same period in 2024, indicating an improvement in per-share performance.
  • Diluted Net Income (Loss) Per Share: $(0.18), compared to $(0.29) in the same period in 2024, showing a similar improvement as the basic EPS.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Aeluma's revenue is primarily derived from government contracts and commercial product and service contracts. For the nine months ended March 31, 2025, $3,147,225 was from government contracts and $200,995 from commercial product and service contracts.
  • New Government Contracts: Aeluma secured an $11.717 million DARPA contract for developing nano-scale semiconductors, with applications in AI, mobile devices, and 5G/6G. The contract is structured with $5.974 million provided over 18 months, and the remaining $5.743 million over the following 18 months as milestones are met.
  • New Government Contracts: On April 24, 2025, Aeluma received a contract from the U.S. Department of Energy to develop low-cost shortwave infrared (SWIR) photodetectors, accelerating commercialization of its wafer-scale platform.
  • Operational Facilities: Aeluma operates a 9,000 sq. ft. facility in Goleta, California, equipped with a state-of-the-art R&D/manufacturing cleanroom and access to rapid prototyping capabilities.
  • Future Outlook: Aeluma plans to continue developing its technology for high-performance semiconductor applications, focusing on scaling production for mass markets and expanding its business development and marketing efforts.
  • Partnerships: Aeluma partners with production-scale fabrication foundries and packaging companies to enhance its manufacturing capabilities and support high-volume market demands.
  • Technology Development: Aeluma's technology is based on the heterogeneous integration of compound semiconductor materials on large-diameter substrates, enabling cost-effective manufacturing of high-performance photodetectors and other devices.
  • Executive Changes: As of March 18, 2025, Mr. James Seo was appointed as interim Chief Financial Officer/Principal Accounting Officer, having served as the Company's Controller since May 2023.

SEC Filing: Aeluma, Inc. [ ALMU ] - 10-Q - May. 09, 2025

data.title: Verizon to speak at J.P. Morgan conference May 14
data.description:
NEW YORK, May 09, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Hans Vestberg, Chairman and CEO of Verizon (NYSE, Nasdaq: VZ), is scheduled to speak at the J.P. Morgan Global Technology, Media and Communications Conference on Wednesday, May 14 at 8:00 a.m. ET. His remarks will be webcast, with access instructions available on Verizon’s Investor Relations website, www.verizon.com/about/investors.

For details on Verizon's most recent financial results, view the company's 1Q25 earnings results here.

Verizon Communications Inc. (NYSE, Nasdaq: VZ) was formed in 2000 and is one of the world’s leading providers of technology and communications services. Headquartered in New York City and with a presence around the world, Verizon generated revenues of $134.0 billion in 2023. The company offers data, video and voice services and solutions on its award-winning networks and platforms, delivering on customers’ demand for mobility, reliable network connectivity, and security.

VERIZON’S ONLINE MEDIA CENTER: News releases, stories, media contacts and other resources are available at verizon.com/news. News releases are also available through an RSS feed. To subscribe, visit www.verizon.com/about/rss-feeds/.

Media contacts:

Adi Wineland

aditya.wineland@verizon.com

data.title: US crude oil futures settle at $61.02
data.description:
US crude oil futures are settling the day at $61.02. That's $1.11 or 1.85%.

For the trading day. The high was at $61.42. The low was at $59.92. The price moved up $3.07 or 4.46% for the trading week but it wasn't until after reaching a low of $55.39 which was just above the low price for the year $55.15 from the week of April 7, and the lowest price going back to February 2021.

Looking at the hourly chart, the price rebound has extended up toward the 38.2% retracement of the range since the April high (high price reached $72.22). The 38.2% retracement comes in at $61.63. Getting above that level is needed to increase the bullish bias from a technical perspective.

On the downside, the 100 and 200-hour moving averages are near each other above and below the $59 level. The 100-hour moving average is starting to move to the upside.

data.title: Wheat Futures Fall Ahead of WASDE - Daily Grain Highlights
data.description:
By Kirk Maltais

-Wheat for July delivery fell 1.6%, to $5.21 a bushel, on the Chicago Board of Trade on Friday, with traders paring risk ahead of Monday's much-anticipated WASDE report from the USDA.

-Corn for July delivery rose 0.5%, to $4.49 3/4 a bushel.

-Soybeans for July delivery rose 0.7%, to $10.52 1/4 a bushel.

HIGHLIGHTS

The Big Kahuna: CBOT grain futures pared gains in preparation for Monday's WASDE, with wheat turning lower as traders prepare for any surprises over the weekend or out of the report next week. Traders are eager to see early forecasts from the USDA as spring planting progresses, as well as information on tariffs and grain demand. "It will be interesting to see whether the USDA takes into account the effects of the tariff conflict on Chinese imports and U.S. exports," said Commerzbank in a note.

Signals From the Top: President Trump set the tone for Friday's trading with a Truth Social post signaling support for reducing the tariff on Chinese goods to 80% from 145%. This comes ahead of weekend meetings between U.S. and Chinese officials in Switzerland, and an overall increasingly optimistic view of trade negotiations going forward, said AgResource in a note, including the U.S.-U.K. trade deal announced Thursday.

INSIGHT

The Next Episode: The U.S.-U.K. trade deal is "one step on a longer journey towards certainty," DWS's George Catrambone said. Now, "heads will turn towards India, Vietnam, Switzerland, Japan. Hopefully those are the next dominoes to fall," he said. As trade deals materialize, "you can start to get some momentum there, that will build certainty." Catrambone says a sequence of such agreements could help improve sentiment after the hectic start of Trump's trade-policy reshuffle, although it could take a while until the full effect of tariffs is reflected in hard data.

Accumulating Shipments: The USDA said this morning that it sold more corn and soybeans to Mexico and Pakistan, building on sales announced yesterday. The agency said that 288,000 metric tons of corn were sold to Mexico, with 95,100 tons for delivery in the current 2024/25 marketing year and the remaining 192,900 tons for 2025/26 delivery. 120,000 tons of soybeans were sold to Pakistan for delivery in 2025/26. Yesterday, the USDA announced sales of 205,000 tons of corn to Mexico and 225,000 tons of soybeans to Pakistan, along with 115,000 tons of corn for delivery to unknown destinations.

AHEAD

  • The USDA will release its weekly Grain Export Inspections report at 11 a.m. ET Monday.
  • The USDA will release its monthly World Agriculture Supply and Demand Estimates report at noon ET Monday.
  • The USDA will release its weekly Crop Progress report at 4 p.m. ET Monday.
  • Paulo Trevisani contributed to this article.

Write to Kirk Maltais at kirk.maltais@wsj.com

data.title: CPS to Host Conference Call on First Quarter 2025 Earnings
data.description:
Las Vegas, Nevada, May 09, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Consumer Portfolio Services, Inc. (“CPS” or the “Company”) today announced that it will hold a conference call on Tuesday, May 13, 2025 at 1:00 p.m. ET to discuss its first quarter 2025 operating results.

Those wishing to participate can pre-register for the conference call at the following link https://register-conf.media-server.com/register/BIa727447d5fdf49d4b7da9c96f3d668b7. Registered participants will receive an email containing conference call details for dial-in options. To avoid delays, we encourage participants to dial into the conference call fifteen minutes ahead of the schedule start time. A replay will be available beginning two hours after conclusion of the call for 12 months via the Company’s website at https://ir.consumerportfolio.com/investor-relations.

About Consumer Portfolio Services, Inc.

Consumer Portfolio Services, Inc. is an independent specialty finance company that provides indirect automobile financing to individuals with past credit problems or limited credit histories. We purchase retail installment sales contracts primarily from franchised automobile dealerships secured by late model used vehicles and, to a lesser extent, new vehicles. We fund these contract purchases on a long-term basis primarily through the securitization markets and service the contracts over their lives.

Investor Relations Contact

Danny Bharwani, Chief Financial Officer

949-753-6811

data.title: Limitless X Holdings Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Limitless X Holdings Inc., a California-based company focused on creating a recession-proof ecosystem integrating health, wellness, entertainment, fintech, community building, and brand development, has released its annual 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: $3.4 million, a decrease of $12.1 million compared to the previous year due to a shift in marketing strategies.

Gross Profit: $2.3 million, representing 67.0% of total revenue, down from $10.8 million and 69.9% of total revenue in the previous year.

Loss from Operations: $(3.9) million, improved from a loss of $(6.3) million in the previous year, primarily due to reduced operating expenses.

Net Loss: $(4.2) million, improved from a net loss of $(13.9) million in the previous year, reflecting gains from debt settlements.

Net Loss Per Common Share - Basic and Diluted: $(0.83), improved from $(3.51) in the previous year.

Business Highlights

Company Overview: Limitless X Holdings Inc. operates through its wholly-owned subsidiary, Limitless X Inc., offering direct-to-consumer e-commerce services with a focus on dietary supplements.

Product Lines: The company's current lead products include NZT-48, NZT-48 Lions Mane, NZT-48 For Her, and Oneshot Nootropic Pre-Workout, designed to enhance cognitive performance, energy, and overall wellness.

New Product Launches: In 2025, Limitless X plans to enter the Consumer Packaged Goods (CPG) space with the launch of a proprietary Coffee Concentrate, designed for taste, convenience, and functional health benefits.

Subsidiary Operations: Limitless X operates several subsidiaries, including Limitless Films, XocelForte Therapeutics, Limitless Entertainment, Limitless Digital Assets, and Limitless Living, each focusing on different aspects of the company's ecosystem.

Future Outlook: The company is optimistic about expanding into new industries such as television and film, regenerative skincare, fintech, entertainment, and real estate, although these endeavors are aspirational and not guaranteed to succeed.

Operational Strategy: Limitless X is leveraging a direct-to-consumer model, focusing on digital marketing and influencer outreach to grow its customer base and brand presence. The company plans to expand its product offerings and increase customer lifetime value through strategic additions and upsell opportunities.

Market Trends: The global health and wellness market is expected to grow significantly, driven by increased health consciousness, technological advancements, and a rise in preventative healthcare. Limitless X aims to capitalize on these trends by expanding its product lines and market reach.

Geographical Expansion: Limitless X plans to expand into key international markets such as Canada, Latin America, and the MENA region, aligning with its mission to make premium health and wellness products accessible worldwide.

Production and Distribution: The company maintains control over its manufacturing process, ensuring high-quality ingredients and reliable supply chain networks. It employs a streamlined omni-channel approach to product distribution, including e-commerce, social media, and retail channels.

Influencer Network: Limitless X utilizes a diverse network of influencers to promote its products, strategically selecting individuals who align with the company's brand message and target audience.

Strategic Initiatives

Marketing Strategy: The company has shifted its marketing strategy from affiliate marketing to in-house digital marketing, aiming to reduce costs and improve efficiency.

Business Expansion: Limitless X plans to expand its business operations into television and film, regenerative skin care, fintech, entertainment, and real estate sectors by April 2025, operating through six active subsidiary companies.

Capital Management: The company has faced significant financial challenges, including a net loss of $4.2 million for the year ended December 31, 2024, and an accumulated deficit of $38.8 million. To address liquidity issues, the company plans to rely on advances from related parties and pursue equity financing through private placements or a registered public offering. The company has also engaged in debt conversion activities, converting vendor debt and accrued salaries into common stock to manage liabilities. No dividends have been paid, and the company does not anticipate paying cash dividends in the foreseeable future.

Future Outlook: The company acknowledges substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern due to recurring net losses and negative cash flows. It plans to address these challenges by raising additional capital and exploring other financing options. The company aims to leverage its new strategic initiatives in various sectors to drive future growth and improve its financial position.

Challenges and Risks

Expansion Risks: The company's diversification strategy into new industries such as television, film, regenerative skin care, fintech, entertainment, and real estate carries significant risks as these ventures may not come to fruition or prove successful.

Financial Risks: The company faces significant financial losses and the expectation of continued losses in the future. The report of the independent registered public accounting firm includes a going concern uncertainty explanatory paragraph, highlighting doubts about the company's ability to continue as a going concern.

Operational Risks: The company is heavily reliant on brands and products owned by its CEO, which could be terminated, leading to a loss of business. Expansion into various industries through subsidiaries presents risks of unsuccessful ventures. The company also faces challenges in acquiring and retaining consumers cost-effectively, and the use of social media and influencers could adversely affect its reputation.

Third-Party Reliance: Reliance on third parties for essential operations and services poses risks of disruptions or failures. Shipping and labor cost increases could negatively impact financial results.

Regulatory Risks: The company is subject to various laws and regulations, and any non-compliance could have adverse effects. The CEO's control over voting securities classifies the company as a 'controlled company,' potentially leading to decisions that may not favor outside shareholders.

Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including economic downturns, inflation, and changes in consumer spending, which could adversely affect revenues. The company also faces risks related to cybersecurity threats, which could result in data breaches and harm its reputation and financial condition. Additionally, the company is subject to risks related to online payment methods and potential increases in transaction costs, which could impact profitability.

SEC Filing: Limitless X Holdings Inc. [ VYBE ] - 10-K - May. 09, 2025

data.title: Kohl's Says Director's Resignation was due to Disagreements
data.description:
Kohl's said Christine Day resigned as a director due to disagreements with the board, the company disclosed Friday in an amended filing.

The retailer previously said that Day's departure was not due to any disagreements with the company.

However, in the latest filing, the company said the disagreements related to how the company responded to the recommendations from the Institutional Shareholder Services, a shareholder advisory firm, on the say-on-pay proposal and board process matters.

"There is simply no way the Board could have interpreted my resignation as having no conflict issues. This was a deliberately selective edit," Day said in her email to the board.

Kohl's said it "strongly disagrees with the assertions in Ms. Day's emails."
